goal.title: Restore native MLX Klein (FLUX.2) generation in mflux
goal.objective: Make `mflux-generate-flux2 --model flux2-klein-4b` produce a correct, prompt-relevant image end-to-end on this machine, natively in MLX, and root-cause every failure found on the way with harness-stamped evidence. As of 2026-07-09 the command fails immediately with model resolution: "Model not found: 'flux2-klein-4b'" (tokenizer_loader._resolve_root_path). Prior notes (2026-06-11) also recorded deeper breakage in the flux2 inference path producing noise output, so expect the resolution failure to be the first layer, not the whole story. Work layer by layer: reproduce, root-cause, fix or precisely characterize, then advance to the next failure until the canary passes.
context: Workspace repo: ~/Dev-Projects/mlx-experiments (formerly known as mlx-mflux; the symlink Image-models/mflux points at mlx-experiments/mflux, so edits here are visible through that path too).

Use the .venv-mlx-fork venv: `.venv-mlx-fork/bin/mflux-generate-flux2` exists there. The .venv-mlx-experimental venv has a STALE mflux install without flux2 entrypoints — do not use it, but its drift may explain older 'broken everywhere' reports.

Reproducible failing command (2026-07-09): `.venv-mlx-fork/bin/mflux-generate-flux2 --model flux2-klein-4b --prompt "a red apple on a wooden table" --steps 4 --seed 42 --width 256 --height 256 --output diagnostics/klein-flux2/probe.png` -> FileNotFoundError: Model not found: 'flux2-klein-4b'.

Klein base weights are NOT in ~/.cache/huggingface/hub (only FLUX.1-dev is). Klein LoRA training previously ran in this repo (training_outputs/klein9b_*, output/flux2_klein_*), so base weights existed at some point — they may have been deleted or live on an external volume. Downloading black-forest-labs/FLUX.2-klein-4B fresh is acceptable; disk and bandwidth are fine.
The flux2 module README (mflux/src/mflux/models/flux2/README.md) documents the supported models and CLI; the documented UX is the target behavior.
Klein 4B is distilled: 4 steps, guidance 1.0 is the standard fast path. Non-distilled base variants need more steps and guidance > 1.0 — do not confuse the two when judging output quality.

Independent oracle for disputed numerical evidence: Kimi K2.7 code thinking via the kimi-k2 plugin / model-gateway MCP tools available in your session. Use it to interpret parity tables, diffs, and upstream reference code blind — not to write patches. A previously used diffusers-based Klein reference venv (referred to as the MOSS venv) may exist on this machine; search for it, and if it is gone, building a minimal diffusers Klein reference in an ISOLATED new venv (e.g. diagnostics/klein-flux2/.venv-ref) is an approved and encouraged independent oracle.
Hardware: Mac Studio M3 Ultra, 512GB unified memory. After every model operation clean up: del model; gc.collect(); mx.metal.clear_cache().
mx.eval is MLX's lazy-computation materializer, not Python's eval — security linters flagging it are false positives.
Write all scratch scripts, probes, and reference outputs under diagnostics/klein-flux2/ in the workspace.
success_criteria: Tier 1 (required): every failure encountered (model resolution, weights, deeper numerical defects) is root-caused with a reproducible command chain, and each decisive observation is harness-stamped via evidence_commands.
Tier 2 (target): the canary gate passes — `mflux-generate-flux2 --model flux2-klein-4b` produces a 256px image that the harness noise-check accepts (structured, non-uniform, non-noise).

Tier 3 (stretch): output verified prompt-relevant at 1024px, and at least one component (text encoder, a transformer block, or the VAE) numerically sanity-checked against an independent reference implementation (diffusers), with shared assumptions of the comparison stated.
Final report documents each failure layer found, its fix or precise characterization, and the exact working invocation.
non_goals: Chroma (weights deleted, model out of date) and every other model family in the repo.
Performance optimization, quantization work, or training-pipeline changes.
Editing anything outside the editable globs; the harness reverts it.
Committing or pushing — the harness owns the git baseline and resets commits.

validation_commands.name: mflux-imports
validation_commands.command: .venv-mlx-fork/bin/python -c "import mflux; from mflux.models.flux2.flux2_initializer import *; print('flux2 imports OK')"

commands.name: klein-generate
commands.command: mkdir -p diagnostics/klein-flux2 && .venv-mlx-fork/bin/mflux-generate-flux2 --model flux2-klein-4b --prompt "a red apple on a wooden table" --steps 4 --seed 42 --width 256 --height 256 --output diagnostics/klein-flux2/canary.png

completion_commands.name: klein-generate-1024
completion_commands.command: .venv-mlx-fork/bin/mflux-generate-flux2 --model flux2-klein-4b --prompt "Photorealistic close-up of a hummingbird hovering near red flowers, frozen wings, detailed feathers" --steps 4 --seed 640563507 --width 1024 --height 576 --output diagnostics/klein-flux2/final.png
